brand.name: ChiLocal
brand.tagline: Chicago, from someone who actually lives here.

note: Sample editorial content in the ChiLocal voice. Addresses are real; coordinates are approximate. Swap for live data from PostGIS — see API.md.

neighborhoods.name: Logan Square
neighborhoods.chilocal_take: Where Chicago's creative class put down roots and refused to leave. Boulevards, a farmers market that takes itself seriously, and the best run of bars in the city that don't try too hard.

neighborhoods.name: Wicker Park
neighborhoods.chilocal_take: Got famous, got expensive, and somehow kept its record stores. Vintage racks, serious cocktails, and people-watching at the six-corners.

neighborhoods.name: Pilsen
neighborhoods.chilocal_take: Wears its Mexican heritage and its art on the same walls. Murals, the city's best carnitas, and galleries that open late the second Friday of the month.

venues.name: Lula Cafe
venues.chilocal_take: The original neighborhood farm-to-table, doing it since before it was a movement. Go for the Monday Farm Dinner — the most thoughtful $55 you'll spend all month.

venues.name: The Whistler
venues.chilocal_take: Cocktail bar, live-music room, and art gallery crammed into one storefront with no sign. Get there before nine or you're standing.
venues.skip_if: You need a table and quiet

venues.name: Longman & Eagle
venues.chilocal_take: A Michelin star and a 400-deep whiskey list pretending to be a roadside inn. Walk-ins only, so come hungry and patient.

venues.name: Reno
venues.chilocal_take: Wood-fired bagels and real coffee by day, a dimmer, different place by night. The morning version is the one to know.
venues.skip_if: You're in a rush at peak weekend

venues.name: Parson's Chicken & Fish
venues.chilocal_take: A patio institution built around fried chicken, fish, and frozen negronis. Go with a group and go early — the yard fills fast.

venues.name: Cellar Door Provisions
venues.chilocal_take: A bakery by day and a tasting-menu room by night, baking some of the best bread in the city. Come early for the pastries.

venues.name: Big Star
venues.chilocal_take: Tacos and bourbon on a patio that's been packed for fifteen years. Weeknights are a great time; the weekend wait is a tax.
venues.skip_if: It's Saturday and you're hungry now

venues.name: The Violet Hour
venues.chilocal_take: An unmarked door and a curtain hide the bar that taught Chicago to take cocktails seriously. Still the standard.
venues.best_for: A real cocktail, a quiet date

venues.name: Myopic Books
venues.chilocal_take: Three floors of used books that have outlasted every trend on Milwaukee Ave. Open late, which is the whole point.

venues.name: Dove's Luncheonette
venues.chilocal_take: Tex-Mex at a counter with a soul record always spinning. Sit at the bar, get the chilaquiles, stay for the pie.

venues.name: Quimby's Bookstore
venues.chilocal_take: The patron saint of zines and weird comics since the '90s — where Wicker Park keeps its counterculture on the shelf.

venues.name: Emporium Arcade Bar
venues.chilocal_take: Pinball, old arcade cabinets, and a beer list far more serious than it needs to be. More fun than it has any right to be.

venues.name: Carnitas Uruapan
venues.chilocal_take: The carnitas benchmark, sold by the pound since 1975. Cash, styrofoam, no apologies — get a half pound and the salsa verde.

venues.name: HaiSous
venues.chilocal_take: Vietnamese cooking with real technique and a courtyard worth lingering in. Order the clay-pot dishes; don't skip the Cà Phê Đá bar next door.

venues.name: Thalia Hall
venues.chilocal_take: An 1892 opera house turned the best-sounding mid-size room in the city, with Dusek's downstairs so you never have to leave.

venues.name: National Museum of Mexican Art
venues.chilocal_take: One of the country's great Mexican art collections, and admission is free. Most of the city still doesn't know that.

venues.name: Pleasant House Pub
venues.chilocal_take: British royal pies done with real care, plus a Sunday roast worth planning around. Cozy and unpretentious.

venues.name: Pilsen Community Books
venues.chilocal_take: A volunteer-run used bookshop that pours its profits back into the neighborhood. Browse slow; buy a couple.
